currentStep.openingLine: Let's compare the positions first instead of trusting how big the numerals look.
currentStep.firstTutorQuestion: Which of these two numbers is farther left on the number line?
currentStep.smallHint: Farther left means smaller, even when the number has a larger absolute value.
currentStep.goodStoppingPoint: Learner orders negative numbers or fractions by position language and not by surface appearance alone.
watchFor: Learner says -7 is greater than -3 because 7 is bigger than 3.
Learner can name endpoints but cannot place a midpoint or a number between two fractions.
currentStep.exampleTutorMove: Sketch a short number line with two labels and ask the learner to place one additional value before comparing.
currentStep.exampleLearnerResponse: -7 is farther left than -3, so it is smaller even though 7 has the larger absolute value.
currentStep.title: Compare and Place on the Number Line
